items.title: Readability
items.avoid: Chaining many itertools calls into one unreadable expression.
items.prefer: Name intermediate steps or use a small generator function.
items.example:
```python
# hard to read
result = list(islice(takewhile(lambda x: x>0, map(score, chain(a,b))), 10))

# clear
scored = (score(x) for x in chain(a, b))
positive = takewhile(lambda x: x > 0, scored)
top10 = list(islice(positive, 10))
```

items.title: Bounding infinite sources
items.prefer: Combine with islice/takewhile to bound them.

items.title: Caching
items.avoid: lru_cache on impure or unbounded-input functions.
items.prefer: Cache pure functions and set a sensible maxsize.
items.why: Avoids stale results and memory growth.
items.example: `@lru_cache(maxsize=2048)` on `tokenize(text)` for a stream of repeating prompts → big speedup. Don't cache `fetch_url(url)` (impure: network can fail/change).

items.title: Folds
items.example: `sum(scores)` over `reduce(operator.add, scores, 0)`. `''.join(chunks)` over `reduce(lambda a,b: a+b, chunks, '')`.
